stem: The graph of y = f(x) in the xy-plane crosses the x-axis at exactly the points (-2, 0) and (5, 0). For how many values of x does f(x) = 0?
rationale: Choice B is correct. A value of x for which f(x) = 0 corresponds to an x-intercept of the graph of y = f(x). The graph has two x-intercepts, at (-2, 0) and (5, 0), so there are two values of x for which f(x) = 0. Choices A, C, and D give incorrect counts.
